targets.description: Documentation surface for framework concepts, guides, and reference material.
Reference app for example compositions built on the framework package and plugin surfaces.
Developer harness for verifying the platform admin workbench, AI operator surfaces, and shell integrations.
Sandbox app for interactive framework experiments and local feature prototyping.
Default universal admin desk plugin.
Durable agent runtime, prompt governance, approval queues, and replay controls.

resources.description: Durable execution record for a governed AI agent run.
resources.businessPurpose: Track agent lifecycle, status, budget use, and replay-safe execution history.
invariants: Each run belongs to one tenant and one agent definition.
Run status changes must remain auditable.
lifecycleNotes: Runs may pause for approval checkpoints before completion.
Replay-safe metadata must remain stable across investigations.

fields.name: agentId
fields.label: Agent
fields.description: Agent definition that owns the run.
fields.businessMeaning: Lets operators group runs by agent purpose and ownership.

fields.name: modelId
fields.label: Model
fields.description: Model profile used for the run.
fields.businessMeaning: Helps audit routing, cost, and quality decisions.

fields.name: startedAt
fields.label: Started
fields.description: Timestamp when the run started execution.
fields.businessMeaning: Supports queue analysis, latency tracking, and investigation timelines.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.description: Current lifecycle status of the agent run.
fields.businessMeaning: Shows whether a run is active, waiting for approval, finished, or failed.

fields.name: stepCount
fields.label: Steps
fields.description: Number of persisted execution steps recorded for the run.
fields.businessMeaning: Indicates how much work the agent performed and how deep the execution went.

resources.description: Approval checkpoint raised by an AI run before a sensitive tool step.
resources.businessPurpose: Make risky agent actions visible, reviewable, and explicitly resolvable by humans.

fields.name: requestedAt
fields.label: Requested
fields.description: Timestamp when human review became required.

fields.name: runId
fields.label: Run
fields.description: Agent run that emitted the approval request.

fields.name: state
fields.label: State
fields.description: Current decision state of the approval request.

fields.name: toolId
fields.label: Tool
fields.description: Requested tool or action awaiting approval.

resources.description: Versioned prompt artifact used for governed AI execution.
resources.businessPurpose: Keep prompt bodies diffable, reviewable, and replay-safe across releases.

fields.name: publishedAt
fields.label: Published
fields.description: Timestamp when the prompt version became available for governed execution.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.description: Publication status of the prompt version.

fields.name: templateId
fields.label: Template
fields.description: Prompt template family that this version belongs to.

fields.name: version
fields.label: Version
fields.description: Human-readable version label for the prompt body.

actions.description: Submit a governed AI run against approved tools and prompt versions.
actions.businessPurpose: Start durable agent work without bypassing tenant, tool, prompt, or replay governance.
preconditions: The tenant, actor, and agent identifiers must be valid.
At least one allowed tool id must be provided.
mandatorySteps: Pin the prompt version before execution begins.
Record replay-safe inputs and tool permissions for the run.
sideEffects: May create an approval checkpoint before completion.
postconditions: A stable run id is returned.
The run status reflects whether approval is needed or execution completed.
failureModes: Validation fails if the prompt version, actor, or tool inputs are incomplete.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not invoke undeclared tools outside the allowed tool list.
Do not run with an unpublished or untracked prompt version.

actions.description: Resolve an AI approval checkpoint with an explicit human decision.
actions.businessPurpose: Allow sensitive AI steps to continue only after accountable human review.
preconditions: The checkpoint must belong to the supplied run and tenant.
The acting user must hold ai.approvals.approve permission.
mandatorySteps: Record whether the checkpoint was approved or rejected.
Preserve the reviewer note whenever one is supplied.
sideEffects: Resumes or terminates the associated run based on the decision.
forbiddenShortcuts: Agents must not self-approve their own checkpoints.

actions.description: Publish a reviewed prompt version for governed use.
actions.businessPurpose: Move prompt changes into an auditable, replay-safe published state before agents depend on them.
preconditions: The prompt body must pass validation and review before publication.
mandatorySteps: Publish prompt versions with a changelog when behavior changes.
Keep prompt versions diffable for later replay and incident review.
sideEffects: Creates a published prompt version record.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not overwrite an existing published prompt body in place.

targets.description: Eval datasets, judges, regression baselines, and release-grade AI review.
Tenant-safe memory collections, retrieval diagnostics, and grounded knowledge pipelines.
Canonical audit trail and sensitive action history.
Canonical identity and session backbone.
Community, groups, and membership backbone.
Pages, posts, and content type backbone.
Dashboard, widget, and saved view backbone.
Document lifecycle and generated document backbone.
File references and storage abstractions.
Dynamic forms and submissions backbone.
Background jobs, schedules, and execution metadata.
Knowledge base, docs, and article tree backbone.
Outbound and in-app notifications.
Tenant and organization graph management.
Layout, block, and builder canvas backbone.
Portal shell and self-service entrypoint backbone.
RBAC and ABAC policy management backbone.
Typed search indexing and query abstractions.
Reusable templates for content, messages, and workflows.
Internal person and directory backbone.
Explicit workflows and approval state machines.

resources.description: Durable workflow instance record used to track approval and publication processes across the platform.
resources.businessPurpose: Give operators and automation a single governed view of every active or historical workflow instance.
invariants: Each workflow instance belongs to one tenant and one workflow definition.
State transitions must remain auditable and replayable.
lifecycleNotes: Instances move between explicit states only through declared transitions.
Approval status and due dates drive operator queues and reminders.

fields.name: approvalStatus
fields.label: Approval
fields.description: Current approval posture for the workflow instance.
fields.businessMeaning: Tells operators whether the instance is waiting on review or already resolved.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.label: Created
fields.description: Creation timestamp for the workflow instance.
fields.businessMeaning: Used for queue ordering, SLA measurement, and audit trails.

fields.name: currentState
fields.label: State
fields.description: Current workflow state for the instance.
fields.businessMeaning: Determines the next allowed transitions and visible operator actions.

fields.name: definitionKey
fields.label: Workflow
fields.description: Workflow template that governs the instance.
fields.businessMeaning: Identifies which state machine and policy rules apply.

fields.name: dueAt
fields.label: Due
fields.description: Optional due date for the current review or approval step.
fields.businessMeaning: Supports escalation, reminders, and queue prioritization.

fields.name: subjectType
fields.label: Subject Type
fields.description: Domain object type that the workflow instance governs.
fields.businessMeaning: Helps operators understand whether the workflow is for content, invoices, or access requests.

actions.description: Apply a governed transition to a workflow instance.
actions.businessPurpose: Move workflows forward in a safe, auditable way while preserving approval and notification side effects.
preconditions: The supplied current state must match the persisted workflow state.
The acting role must be allowed to perform the requested transition.
mandatorySteps: Evaluate the workflow definition before applying the transition.
Emit audit metadata and workflow side effects after each successful transition.
sideEffects: Returns notification and follow-up instructions for downstream systems.
Produces an audit event type tied to the transition.
postconditions: The returned next state is always valid for the workflow definition.
Approval status reflects the new workflow state.
failureModes: Transition fails if the current state and requested transition are incompatible.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not patch workflow state directly in storage.
Do not skip approval-driven transitions just because an admin initiated the request.

workflows.description: Review and grant sensitive access only after security review.
workflows.businessPurpose: Protect privileged access changes with explicit security approval and revocation paths.
invariants: Access cannot be granted without a security review.
mandatorySteps: Every access request starts in draft and must be submitted for review before approval.
Revocation must remain available after a grant.

draft.description: The access request is being prepared by the requester.

security_review.description: Security is evaluating the request and its risk posture.

granted.description: Access has been granted and is actively in force.

rejected.description: The request was denied and may be reopened for correction.

revoked.description: Previously granted access has been removed.

transitionDescriptions.draft.submit: Sends the request into security review.
transitionDescriptions.security_review.reject: Rejects the request and closes the review cycle.
transitionDescriptions.granted.revoke: Removes access after it has been granted.
transitionDescriptions.rejected.reopen: Returns the request to draft for correction and resubmission.

workflows.description: Editorial review and publication workflow for governed content.
workflows.businessPurpose: Prevent content from reaching publication without editorial review and scheduled release control.
invariants: Published content must pass through editor review.
Archived content cannot be republished without reopening.
mandatorySteps: Draft content must be submitted for editor review.
Scheduled content must pass through publish before becoming live.

draft.description: Content is being prepared by the author.

editor_review.description: Editors are checking readiness, accuracy, and compliance.

scheduled.description: Content is approved and waiting for publication.

published.description: Content is live and visible to the intended audience.

rejected.description: Editorial review failed and the content must be revised.

archived.description: Content is no longer active but remains in history.

transitionDescriptions.draft.submit: Moves the draft into editorial review.

workflows.description: Finance approval flow for invoices before they are finalized.
workflows.businessPurpose: Ensure invoices are reviewed before final approval and archival.
invariants: Invoices cannot move to approved without a pending approval state.
Rejected invoices must reopen before resubmission.
mandatorySteps: Invoice drafts must be submitted before approvers can act.
Only approved invoices may be archived.

draft.description: Invoice is being prepared and has not yet entered approval.

pending_approval.description: Invoice is waiting for finance approval.

approved.description: Invoice has been approved and is ready for finalization.

rejected.description: Invoice was rejected and must be corrected.

archived.description: Invoice has been finalized and archived.
